questions.question: A coil of self inductance $0.5\text{ mH}$ carries a current of $2\text{A}$. The energy stored in Joule is
What is the self inductance of a coil which produces self induced emf of $5\text{ V}$, when the current changes from $3\text{ A}$ to $2\text{ A}$ in one millisecond?
The north pole of a magnet is falling on a metallic ring as shown in the figure. The direction of induced current, if looked from upside in the ring will be [Figure]

options.C: Clock-wise or anticlock-wise depending on metal of the ring.

questions.question: X and Y, two metallic coils are arranged in such a way that, when steady change in current flowing in X coil is 4 A, change in magnetic flux associated with coil Y is 0.4 Wb. Mutual inductance of the system of these coils is ....... H.

A conducting ring of radius r is placed perpendicularly inside a time varying magnetic field given by $B = B_0 + \alpha t$, as shown in the figure. $B_0$ and $\alpha$ are positive constants. Find emf produced in the ring. [Figure]
Magnetic Flux linked with a coil is $\phi = 5t^2 + 2t + 3$, where $t$ is in second and $\phi$ is in weber. At time $t = 1 \text{ sec.}$, the value of induced emf is ........... volt.
Magnetic flux of $10\text{ }\mu\text{ Wb}$ is linked with a coil, when a current of $2\text{ mA}$ flows through it. What is the self inductance of the coil?
What is the self inductance of a solenoid of length $31.4\text{ cm}$, area of cross section $10^{-3}\text{m}^2$ and total number of turns $10^3$ ?
The dimensional formula of magnetic flux is ________.

A conducting Coil having $500$ turns has cross - sectional area $0.15 \text{ m}^2$. A magnetic field of strength $0.2 \text{ T}$ linked perpendicular to this area changes to $1.0 \text{ T}$ in $0.4 \text{ sec}$. The induced emf produced in the coil will be ________ volt.

The Network shown in Figure is a part of the circuit. (The battery has negligible resistance) [Diagram]. At a certain instant the current $I = 2\text{ A}$ and it is decreasing at the rate of $10^2\text{ As}^{-1}$. What is the potential difference between the points B and A?

A rod of $10\text{ cm}$ length is moving perpendicular to uniform magnetic field of intensity $5 \times 10^{-4}\text{ Wb/m}^2$. If the acceleration of the rod is $5\text{ m/s}^2$, then the rate of increase of induced emf is ________.

The number of turns in the coil of an A.C. generator are 100 and its cross-sectional area is 2.5 $m^2$. The coil is revolving in a uniform magnetic field of strength 0.3 T with the uniform angular velocity of 60 rad/s. The value of maximum value produced is _____ kV.
If $R$ and $L$ denote resistance and inductance respectively which of the following has dimension of time?

Two inductors each of inductance $L$ are connected in parallel. One more inductor of value $5\text{ mH}$ is connected in series of this configuration then the effective inductance is $15\text{ mH}$. The value of $L$ is ___ $\text{mH}$.

A coil having $200$ turns has a surface area of $0.15\text{ m}^2$. A magnetic field of strength $0.2\text{ T}$ applied perpendicular to this changes to $0.6\text{ T}$ in $0.4\text{ s}$, then the induced emf in the coil is ___ $\text{V}$.

A wheel of radius $2 \text{ m}$ having 8 conducting concentric spokes is rotating about its geometrical axis with an angular velocity of $10 \text{ rad/s}$ in a uniform magnetic field of $0.2 \text{ T}$ perpendicular to its plane. The value of induced emf between the rim of the wheel and centre is ..................V

A coil of surface area $200 \text{ cm}^2$ having 25 turns is held perpendicular to the magnetic field of intensity $0.02 \frac{\text{Wb}}{\text{m}^2}$. The resistance of the coil is $1\Omega$. If it is removed from the magnetic field in $1 \text{ s}$, the induced charge in the coil is ................C.

The coil having $1000$ turns & Area of $0.10\text{ m}^2$ rotates at half a revolution per second & it is placed in a uniform magnetic field of $0.01\text{ T}$ perpendicular to the axis of rotation of coil. Then max. emf voltage generated in coil is ...... $\text{V}$
Out of the following given loops in which loop, the direction of induced current is from a c b. [Diagram]
Which is not the unit of Inductance?
For a long current carrying solenoid having inside magnetic field is $0.6\text{ T}$. Then find the magnetic energy per unit volume is __________.
The self inductance $L$ of a solenoid of length $l$ and area of cross-section $A$ increase __________ . (Here, with fixed number of turns $N$).

A pair of adjacent coils has a mutual inductance of $1.5\text{ H}$. If the current in one coil changes from $0$ to $20\text{ A}$ in $0.5\text{ sec}$. what is the change of flux linkage with the other coil?

An air-cored solenoid with length $30\text{ cm}$, area of cross-section $25\text{ cm}^2$ and number of turns $500$, carries a current of $2.5\text{ A}$. The current is suddenly switched off in a brief time of $10^{-3}\text{ s}$. How much is the average back emf induced across the ends of the open switch in the circuit? Ignore the variation in magnetic field near the ends of the solenoid.
"The polarity of induced emf is such that it tends to produce a current which opposes the change in magnetic flux that produced it." This statement is known as ________.

A pair of adjacent coils has a mutual inductance of $1.5\text{ H}$. If the current in one coil changes from $0$ to $10\text{ A}$ in $0.5\text{ s}$, what is the change of flux linkage with the other coil?
The dimensional formula of self inductance is ________.
As shown in the figure a bar magnet is moving towards a stationary coil with constant speed v. The direction of induced current in the coil observed by the observer on R.H.S. is ________. [Figure]

options.D: Induced current will not be produced

questions.question: A circular coil of area $2 \text{ cm}^2$ is placed in a magnetic field of 3T perpendicularly. The coil has 10 turns and $5 \, \Omega$ resistance. Now the coil is removed from magnetic field in 0.2 s. The value of induced charge flowing through the coil is ________.

A square loop of side $10\text{ cm}$ and resistance $0.5\Omega$ is placed vertically in the east-west plane. A uniform magnetic field of $0.10\text{T}$ is setup across the plane in the north-east direction. The magnetic field is decreased to zero in $0.70\text{ S}$ at a steady rate. Then the magnitude of induced current during this time interval will be ________.
A coil has N turns and current passes through it is I ampere then we obtain L Henry of self inductance. Now if current charge to $5\text{I}$ then new self inductance will be ________ H.

As shown in figure two identical conducting rings of radius $r$ are placed in magnetic field. In figure(a) magnetic field increasing at the rate of $0.3 \text{ T/s}$ and in figure(b) magnetic field decreasing at the rate of $0.2 \text{ T/s}$. The direction of current in ring (a) and ring(b), when observe from top are ________. [Figure]
A pair of adjacent coils has a mutual inductance of 2H. If the current in one coil changes from 0 to 30A in 0.15s, what is the change of flux linkage with the other coil?
